# datadog_metric_tag_configuration (Data Source)

Use this data source to retrieve the tag configuration for a metric, or to test whether one exists. Unlike most Datadog data sources, this one returns successfully when the metric has no tag configuration; in that case `exists` is `false` and the other computed attributes are unset. This is intended for use with `for_each` and `import {}` blocks driven by dynamic discovery, where some discovered metrics may not have a tag configuration yet.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metric_name` (String) The metric for which to fetch the tag configuration.

### Read-Only

- `exclude_tags_mode` (Boolean) If `true`, the `tags` list is treated as an exclude list rather than an include list. Null when `exists` is `false` or the field is not set.
- `exists` (Boolean) Whether a tag configuration exists for this metric.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `include_percentiles` (Boolean) Whether percentile aggregations are configured. Only meaningful for distribution metrics. Null when `exists` is `false` or the field is not set.
- `metric_type` (String) The metric type. One of `gauge`, `count`, `rate`, `distribution`. Empty when `exists` is `false`.
- `tags` (List of String) The list of queryable tag keys. Empty when `exists` is `false`.
